title: AI fakes a growing problem on Apple Books, some ‘written’ in a few hours
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

* **Core Story/Main Event:**
 + AI-generated fake content is becoming a growing problem on Apple Books, with some "written" in a matter of hours.
* **Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
 + Joanna Stern (tech writer)
 + Kashmir Hill (New York Times journalist)
 + Amazon
 + Apple
* **Complete List of Products/Features:**
 + None mentioned in the text (the focus is on fake content, not specific products or features)
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
 + None mentioned in the text
* **Direct Quotes:**
 + None mentioned in the text
* **Additional Facts:**
 + Joanna Stern discovered 10 fake versions of her book "I Am Not a Robot" on Apple Books within days of its publication.
 + New fake versions of her book appeared after she was able to get the initial ones removed.
 + Kashmir Hill found a fake biography of herself on Amazon, which was one of thousands of AI-written biographies available for sale.

  - Apple Renaissance at Colony Park in Ridgeland, Mississippi:

  - Apple Briarwood in Ann Arbor, Michigan:
    - Relocating to a new space at the mall on Friday, July 31 at 10 a.m. local time
    - Entrance to the new, larger store will be in front of The Lawn, a new outdoor space at the mall
  - Apple The Promenade Shops at Briargate in Colorado Springs, Colorado:
    - Moving six doors down to the left on Friday, July 31 at 10 a.m. local time

- Jony Ive delivered a tribute at Steve Jobs' memorial service in 2011.
- Jony Ive is reportedly not closely involved in Open AI's day-to-day recruiting or engineering.

title: Apple Shelved Two Mac Pro Models, Including an Intel-Based One
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

* **Core Story/Main Event:**
 + Apple developed two unreleased Mac Pro models, codenamed J170 and J190, that never made it to market.
 + The Mac Pro was discontinued this year, ending its 20-year run.
* **Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
 + Mark Gurman (Bloomberg)
 + Apple
 + Intel Corp.
* **Complete List of Products/Features:**
 + J170: Intel-based Mac Pro (never released)
 + J190: M3 Ultra-equipped Mac Pro (never released)
 + M3 Ultra Mac Studio (released in early 2025)
 + M2 Ultra Mac Pro (released in 2023)
 + M5 Ultra Mac Studio (expected to be released this fall)
 + "Extreme" chip variants (processors with twice the CPU and GPU cores of Apple's Ultra chips, developed for a future Mac Pro but canceled due to cost and demand concerns)
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
 + The J170 Intel-based Mac Pro was priced roughly double that of a similarly configured Mac Studio.
 + The M3 Ultra Mac Studio was released in early 2025.
 + The M2 Ultra Mac Pro was released in 2023.
 + The M5 Ultra Mac Studio is expected to be released this fall.
 + The Mac Pro was discontinued this year.
* **Direct Quotes:**
 + "I do not expect Apple to bring back the Mac Pro 'anytime soon — if ever.'" - Mark Gurman

title: 'ClickLock' Malware Coerces Mac Users Into Giving Up Passwords
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

* **Core Story/Main Event:**
 + A new piece of mac OS malware, "Click Lock Stealer," has been identified by Security firm Group-IB.
 + The malware pressures users into surrendering their passwords via fake system prompts.
* **Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
 + Group-IB (Security firm)
 + Apple (mac OS developer)
 + Cloudflare (browser verification service)
 + Opera (browser developer)
* **Complete List of Products/Features:**
 + Click Lock Stealer (malware)
 + Terminal (mac OS command-line interface)
 + Cloudflare check or browser verification step (fake page)
 + Click Fix page (fake page)
 + Chrome's "Safe Storage" AES key (encryption key)
 + Keychain item (mac OS password storage)
 + i Cloud process (disguised backdoor)
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
 + No pricing information available
 + The campaign has been active since May 2026
 + At least 100 victims across 33 countries
 + More than half of the victims were in Europe
* **Direct Quotes:**
 + "Such pages instruct visitors to copy a command and run it in Terminal as a supposed 'verification requirement.'" (Group-IB)
 + "No legitimate website will ever ask a user to paste a command into Terminal." (Group-IB)
* **Additional Information:**
 + The malware needs no exploits and no elevated privileges to work.
 + The attack depends on the victim pasting a command into Terminal and running it.
 + The command then executes a script, and everything else follows.
 + The script discreetly downloads several modules and shows a terminal-based loading animation mimicking a Cloudflare progress bar with browser verification.
 + If a user declines the initial password dialog that appears, the malware starts locking system usage.
 + The malware kills every visible app every 210 milliseconds while the password prompt remains on screen.
 + The rest of the desktop stays unusable until the victim gives in.
 + Another loop suppresses mac OS security notifications for roughly six hours.
 + If the user gives up and enters their password, a second, genuine mac OS prompt is subsequently forced to the front, asking them to allow access to a Keychain item.
 + Granting it hands over Chrome's "Safe Storage" AES key.
 + Click Lock then harvests browser credentials, Keychain data, password manager vaults, and any cryptocurrency wallets, and then sends it all to a Telegram bot.
 + It also installs a hidden backdoor disguised as an i Cloud process to give it continual access.
 + Apple has already updated mac OS to try to defend against this class of attack before it can get underway.
 + In mac OS Tahoe 26.4, it added a warning that appears when the user attempts to paste a command into Terminal from a website, chat, or message, and blocks the paste until the user reviews it.
 + In cases where mac OS detects known malware, the paste is blocked outright with no override.

title: Apple's Secret Siri Interface Buried in macOS 27 Beta: How to Enable
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

* **Core Story/Main Event:**
 + Apple is testing a hidden popover interface for Siri AI in the macOS 27 Golden Gate beta.
* **Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
 + Apple
 + Reddit (r/Mac OSBeta community)
 + Mac Rumors.com
* **Complete List of Products/Features:**
 + Writing Tools
 + Contextual actions
 + Rewrite
 + Proofread
 + "How does this sound?"
 + "Edit with Siri"
 + Creating key points
 + Summarizing text
 + Adding contact details
 + Composing a message or email
 + Creating a calendar event
 + Showing a location in Maps
 + Tracking flights
 + Tracking packages
* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
 + No pricing information
 + macOS 27 Golden Gate beta release date: fall (no specific date mentioned)
* **Direct Quotes:**
 + None
* **Other Key Facts:**
 + The Siri popover is disabled by default in macOS 27 developer beta 3 and public beta 1.
 + Beta testers can switch on the feature through a private feature flag.
 + Some actions don't respond, suggesting Apple is still building out the underlying functionality.
 + The UI's behavior is inconsistent.
 + The interface may not make it to the final release of macOS 27 Golden Gate.
* **Terminal Commands:**
 + To enable the interface: `sudo mkdir -p /Library/Preferences/Feature Flags/Domain && sudo defaults write /Library/Preferences/Feature Flags/Domain/Writing Tools Lightweight UI_mac OS -dict Enabled -bool true`
 + To disable the interface: `sudo defaults write /Library/Preferences/Feature Flags/Domain/Writing Tools Lightweight UI_mac OS -dict Enabled -bool false`

title: Bristol Myers Squibb Building Life Science Industry’s Most Advanced AI Factory on NVIDIA Vera Rubin
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

**Core Story/Main Event:**
- Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) is deploying its second NVIDIA DGX Super POD, built on eight DGX Vera Rubin NVL72 systems, to create the life science industry's most advanced AI factory.

**Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
- Erin Davis, Vice President of Research Business Insights and Technology at BMS
- Payal Sheth, Senior Vice President of Therapeutic Discovery Sciences at BMS
- Greg Meyers, Chief Digital and Technology Officer at BMS
- NVIDIA (company)
- Bristol Myers Squibb (BMS) (company)
- Chem Axon (company)
- Schrödinger (company)
- X-Chem (company)

**Complete List of Products/Features:**
- NVIDIA DGX Super POD
- NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 systems
- NVIDIA Bio Ne Mo Agent Toolkit for biological AI
- CELMo D compounds (molecules engineered to selectively degrade cancer-causing proteins)
- Predict First methodology (lead optimization stages of drug discovery)
- Computational model of a molecule's structure
- Agentic workflows
- NVIDIA Mission Control
- Unified AI platform
- Unified data plane
- AI-native tooling
- Digital twins

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing or financial numbers mentioned in the text.

**Direct Quotes:**
- "Instead of equipping a small group of researchers with access to the supercomputer, we're opening it up to literally every scientist," - Erin Davis
- "We use predictions as a way to prioritize synthesis of molecules with multi parameter optimization," - Payal Sheth
- "We're saturated," - Erin Davis
- "It's not the technology, The challenge is how to get that into the hands of actual scientists and learn from it," - Erin Davis
- "Welcome to Limitless Compute," - Erin Davis
- "The compute infrastructure is what connects all of our scientists together and ensures that our learnings are institutionalized," - Payal Sheth
- "There's a cumulative learning loop today in drug discovery that did not exist when I first started my career," - Payal Sheth
- "Agents don't care, They go all across. And that is a huge game-changer because now we can learn from decisions across the silos and across programs," - Erin Davis
- "When you as a scientist can go to an army of well-vetted, fully trained virtual scientists that have BMS knowledge baked in now you're a whole team in and of yourself," - Erin Davis
- "You still have to have that human brain driving things, still looking for caveats and gotchas, still teaching them how to utilize knowledge. But this takes up the capabilities of individual humans substantially," - Erin Davis
- "Just give us time," - Erin Davis

title: How long is a new iPhone actually supposed to last?
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

**Core Story/Main Event:**
- The article discusses the lifespan of a new iPhone, specifically how long it is supposed to last before needing to be replaced.

**Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
- Apple
- Adnan Ahmed (for Engadget)
- vfhnb12 (Shutterstock)

**Complete List of Products/Features:**
- iPhone 11 (2019 model)
- iPhone 15 series (with 80% battery capacity after 1,000 charge cycles)
- iPhone 15 Pro Max (with 83% battery health and 950 charge cycles)
- iPhone 16 or iPhone 17 model (expected to be well-supported through at least 2031)
- iPhone 6s (supports WhatsApp with iOS 15.1 or newer)
- iPhone 17 Pro series (with unibody aluminum build)
- iPhone with Liquid Glass (renders transparency effects in real-time)
- iPhone with Camera Control (locked behind newer iPhone models)
- iPhone with customizable Siri AI (locked behind newer iPhone models)
- iOS 15.1 or newer (required for WhatsApp functionality)
- iOS 26 (taxing on older hardware)
- iOS 27 (early beta build with performance and stability improvements)

**Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
- No specific pricing information is mentioned in the article.
- The article mentions that Apple has hiked prices for its iPads and Mac computers due to tighter memory supplies.

**Direct Quotes:**
- "Apple's commitment to long-term software support has been unmatched for a long time." (no specific source mentioned)
- "If your iPhone's battery health dips below 80 percent, you will be prompted to get it replaced." (no specific source mentioned)

**Additional Facts:**
- Apple's software support history suggests that a newer iPhone 16 or iPhone 17 model should be well-supported through at least 2031.
- Most third-party apps don't instantly drop support for outdated versions of operating systems.
- The "Batterygate" controversy resulted in multi-million dollar class-action lawsuits, and Apple has since made performance management and battery health metrics more transparent.
- Public sentiment around iPhone longevity is positive, with many users holding onto their iPhones for five years or more with only a battery replacement needed.
- Apple indirectly acknowledged performance complaints surrounding iOS 26 by spending most of the iOS 27 reveal talking about performance and stability improvements.

title: Avengers: Doomsday finally has a real trailer
summary:
Here's the extracted information in a clean, structured bulleted list:

* **Core Story / Main Event:**
  + Avengers: Doomsday is a comic book team-up movie featuring a large ensemble of Marvel heroes.
  + The movie is set to be released in theaters on December 18.
  + The story involves a misunderstanding in the first act, leading to heroes fighting each other instead of the main antagonist.
  + The movie is likely to be the final bow for the 2000s-era X-Men, led by Patrick Stewart.
  + It could also be the curtain call for Chris Evans as Captain America.

* **Important Names, Companies, Brands, or Key People:**
  + Marvel
  + Chris Evans (playing Captain America)
  + Robert Downey Jr. (playing Doctor Doom)
  + Patrick Stewart (leading the 2000s-era X-Men)
  + Thor
  + Black Panther
  + Shang-Chi
  + Loki
  + Ant-Man
  + Two Captain Americas

* **Complete List of Products/Features:**
  + None (this article is not about products or features, but rather a movie trailer and release information)

* **Pricing, Release Dates, or Financial Numbers:**
  + Avengers: Doomsday is set to be released in theaters on December 18.
  + Avengers: Secret Wars is set to be released on December 17, 2027.

* **Direct Quotes:**
  + None (there are no direct quotes in the provided text)

* **Additional Information:**
  + The trailer features the X-Men, Thunderbolts, and the Fantastic Four, in addition to other Marvel heroes.
  + The movie is the first Avengers film since Endgame, which was released in 2019.
  + The trailer includes footage of Chris Evans as Captain America, who took a zero-movie break from the franchise after becoming an old man and disappearing into the ether of time in Endgame.
  + Robert Downey Jr. is returning to the fold, playing the primary antagonist Doctor Doom, but there isn't any footage of him out of the costume in the trailer.
